text:
## Escalation sweep complete.

**Hot services detected:** 4
- api-gateway: 7 unresolved issues
- billing: 7 unresolved issues  
- mobile-bff: 7 unresolved issues
- web-frontend: 8 unresolved issues

**Actions taken:**
- ✅ Acknowledged **5 PagerDuty incidents** (PD-407, PD-416, PD-421, PD-424, PD-433)
- ✅ Sent escalation email to oncall@acme.io with hot service summary
I'll start by checking the Sentry issues and finding hot

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT project, COUNT(*) as unresolved_count FROM sentry_issues WHERE status = 'unresolved' GROUP BY project HAVING COUNT(*) > 5"}

text:
There are **4 hot services**: billing (7 issues), web-frontend (8), api-gateway (7), and mobile-bff (7).

Now I'll check for PagerDuty incidents in 'triggered' status for these hot services and

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT id, service FROM pagerduty_incidents WHERE status = 'triggered' AND service IN ('billing', 'web-frontend', 'api-gateway', 'mobile-bff')"}

text: Found **5 triggered PagerDuty incidents** for hot services. Now I'll acknowledge all of them and send the escalation email:

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"BEGIN;

-- Acknowledge all triggered incidents for hot services
UPDATE pagerduty_incidents SET status = 'acknowledged' 
WHERE status = 'triggered' 
AND service IN ('billing', 'web-frontend', 'api-gateway', 'mobile-bff');

-- Send escalation email
INSERT INTO emails (to_addr, subject, body) VALUES 
('oncall@acme.io', 'Escalation', 
'Hot services with >5 unresolved Sentry issues:
…(+110)
